title: SoundHound AI (SOUN) Q4 2024 Earnings Call Transcript
text: Q4 revenue reached $34.5 million, up 101% year-over-year. Full-year 2024 revenue hit $85 million, representing 85% growth. Company achieved cumulative subscriptions and bookings backlog of nearly $1.2 billion, up 75% annually. CEO Keyvan Mohajer noted that revenue increased by eight times and queries increased by seven times since the company went public less than three years ago. The company introduced its third business pillar—voice commerce—which merges product discovery and transactions. Following CES demonstrations, multiple automakers are pursuing pilots: two Japanese automakers in POC phase, two German automakers in POC phase, and several U.S. and Korean OEMs pursuing evaluations. Customer concentration significantly improved, with the largest customer representing 14% of 2024 revenue versus nearly 50% in 2023. The company expanded into new sectors, including energy, government, and military applications. Management raised full-year guidance to $157-$177 million in revenue and expects adjusted EBITDA profitability by year-end.
